summary: Iter 6 — BOTH backend + frontend tested. Backend: 19/19 pytest passing (test_iter6_ebook_and_video.py). Covered: GET /api/ebook (31 chapters, 7 parts, title/subtitle/author), GET /api/ebook/{slug} (content_md + metadata, known-slug sanity: parte1-01-frame, 00-00-introduccion, parte1-02-iteracion present, 404 on unknown, 403 unenrolled), GET /api/ebook.pdf (content-type application/pdf, Content-Disposition attachment, 1.73MB, pypdf reported 150+ pages well above the >=30 threshold, cover page contains 'Prompts que funcionan' after whitespace-normalization — PDF text extraction splits the title glyphs during kerning so direct `in` check must normalize), gate (401 no token, 403 unenrolled student, 200 enrolled + admin bypass), PATCH /admin/module with video_youtube_id set/clear (including regression that GET /course/ia-ele/content exposes the field after PATCH), non-admin 403, regression PUT /auth/profile + /dashboard + /resource/teach-preview + /course/teach-preview/task/{id}. Frontend (Playwright 1440x900 + 400x844): all scenarios pass — (1) Dashboard shows 📘 Mi libro link for enrolled student, (2) /libro page renders banner + Descargar PDF button + 7 parts + 31 chapter cards, (3) Authenticated fetch to /api/ebook.pdf returns 200 + application/pdf + attachment filename 'prompts-que-funcionan.pdf' + 1.73MB, (4) /libro/parte1-01-frame shows sidebar (31 items, active item highlighted), breadcrumb 'Mi área › Prompts que funcionan › Anatomía de un prompt eficaz', ebook-chapter-body rendered (~10KB text), pager-next href points to parte1-02-iteracion, clicking next updates active sidebar item + URL + scrolls top, (5) Mobile 400px viewport: desktop sidebar hidden, '📚 Índice del libro' drawer button visible and opens drawer, (6) Admin panel: admin-module-video-edit-1 opens input, save persists id (btn text updates to '🎥 YT: dQw4w9WgXcQ'), clear empties and returns to '🎥 Añadir vídeo YouTube', (7) With video set, ModuleDetail iframe renders src exactly 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/dQw4w9WgXcQ?rel=0&modestbranding=1'; after clearing video, wrapper no longer rendered. Cleaned up: video_youtube_id on mod-ia-01 reverted to None at end of run.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py ebook gate (_ensure_any_enrollment, lines 1443-1452): requires status='active' AND payment_status='paid'. The enrolled_student fixture in the legacy conftest only sets status='active' — iter6 tests add payment_status='paid' explicitly. Older conftest fixture should be updated if later tests rely on ebook gating (non-blocking).

server.py download_ebook_pdf (line 1501): WeasyPrint is invoked synchronously inside an async handler — for a 1.7MB / 150-page render this takes ~3-5s and blocks the event loop. Consider wrapping in asyncio.to_thread or a worker for production scale.

server.py admin_update_module (line 1278-1280): video_youtube_id is stored as-is without regex validation. Any 11-char YouTube ID format is accepted silently; malformed strings like '<script>' would be embedded verbatim into the iframe src. Recommend validating against r'^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{11}$' server-side before persisting.

Module.video_youtube_id is returned by /course/{slug}/content only when the field exists in Mongo — absent keys show up as 'missing' in the API response (not null). Frontend uses truthy check so both are safe, but strict clients consuming the schema may expect the key to always be present. Consider normalizing to explicit null via clean_doc.

EbookChapter.jsx breadcrumb builds 'Mi área › Prompts que funcionan › {title}' but the first crumb is plain text (no link). Consider making it a <Link to='/dashboard'> for consistency with other pages.
test_credentials: Admin: benitezl@go.ugr.es (via make_magic_token + POST /api/auth/verify). Students minted per-test: TEST_iter6_enroll_*, TEST_iter6_unenroll_*, TEST_fe_iter6_*@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Inserted fresh paid enrollments (status=active, payment_status=paid) directly into Mongo for the newly-created iter6 test students. Temporarily set video_youtube_id='dQw4w9WgXcQ' on mod-ia-01 during PATCH and iframe tests — reverted to None (empty-string PATCH) at end of each flow. No residue on module 01.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 6 adds the ebook (31 chapters / 7 parts) and YouTube video per module. Ebook slug pattern: {part_key}-{filename_stem} e.g. '00-00-introduccion', 'parte1-01-frame', 'parte1-02-iteracion', 'apendices-a-banco-prompts'. Ebook gate checks BOTH status=='active' AND payment_status=='paid' — older tests may need to pass payment_status='paid' in the enrollments.insert_one(). Admin bypasses the gate. PDF is generated via WeasyPrint in-process (~3-5s, 1.73MB, 150+ pages). For iframe tests, use 'dQw4w9WgXcQ' and always clean up with PATCH /admin/module/{id} {video_youtube_id:''}. Known testids: dashboard-ebook-link, ebook-banner, ebook-download-pdf, ebook-part-{N}, ebook-chapter-{slug}, ebook-sidebar-desktop, ebook-sidebar-nav, ebook-sidebar-item-{slug}, ebook-breadcrumb, ebook-chapter-body, ebook-pager, ebook-pager-prev, ebook-pager-next, ebook-open-drawer, ebook-drawer, ebook-close-drawer, admin-module-video-edit-{order}, admin-module-video-input-{order}, admin-module-video-save-{order}, module-video (wrapper div; iframe is child). The PDF fetch must be done via authenticated fetch+blob — page.goto does NOT work due to missing auth header.
rca of the issue: No iter6 regressions or bugs found. All 19 pytest + 7 Playwright scenarios pass. Two non-blocking code-quality observations logged: (a) WeasyPrint sync call should be offloaded to a thread for prod, (b) video_youtube_id accepts arbitrary strings — add regex validation.
